summary: notify-theme fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com via detached child process
details: notify-theme impersonates the pino logger — it ships pino's file layout (lib/proto.js, lib/levels.js, lib/multistream.js, lib/transport.js, lib/redaction.js, lib/tools.js, lib/worker.js), exports `module.exports.pino = middleware`, and reuses pino's keywords ('fast','logger','stream','json') to lure consumers into wiring it into their apps. When the exported middleware is invoked, index.js spawns lib/caller.js as a detached child with `stdio: 'ignore'` and `child.unref()`, hiding output from the caller. lib/caller.js then fetches JavaScript from https://jsonkeeper.com/b/TJKON via axios and executes the response body through `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)`, granting the fetched code full Node privileges including `require`. lib/const.js contains a base64-encoded alternate loader URL (decodes to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/ZK45J) and a custom header name ('x-secret-key'), providing a rotated endpoint. Because jsonkeeper.com is a public paste-style host, the operator can rotate the payload at any time to execute arbitrary code on any machine that loads a consumer of this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
